title: rubus furvus subsp. notabilis
intro: Rubus furvus subsp. notabilis, also known as the Notable Blackberry or the Black Raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus furvus plant, which is a type of bramble in the rose family. It is a thorny shrub native to parts of Asia, known for its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. Herbal preparations of Rubus furvus subsp. notabilis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are used to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health.
